You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines repository.
The codebase is at version 1.6 (commit: 1ed19c87).

## Issue to Resolve

Consider deprecating or changing the behaviour of CoroutineContext.isActive
According to the [doc](https://kotlin.github.io/kotlinx.coroutines/kotlinx-coroutines-core/kotlinx.coroutines/is-active.html), `isActive` has the following property:

>The coroutineContext.isActive expression is a shortcut for coroutineContext[Job]?.isActive == true. See [Job.isActive](https://kotlin.github.io/kotlinx.coroutines/kotlinx-coroutines-core/kotlinx.coroutines/-job/is-active.html).

It means that, if the `Job` is not present, `isActive` always returns `false`.

We have multiple reports that such behaviour can be error-prone when used with non-`kotlinx.coroutines` entry points, such as Ktor and `suspend fun main`, because it is inconsistent with the overall contract:

>(Job) has not been completed and was not cancelled yet


`CoroutineContext.isActive` predates both `CoroutineScope` (which should always have a `Job` in it, if it's not `GlobalScope`)  and `job` extension, so it may be the case that it can be safely deprecated.

Basically, we have three options:

* Do nothing, left things as is. It doesn't solve the original issue, but also doesn't introduce any potentially breaking changes
* Deprecate `CoroutineContext.isActive`. Such change has multiple potential downsides
    * Its only possible replacement is `this.job.isActive`, but this replacement is not equivalent to the original method -- `.job` throws an exception for contexts without a `Job`. An absence of replacement can be too disturbing as [a lot of code](https://grep.app/search?q=context.isActive&filter[lang][0]=Kotlin) rely on a perfectly fine `ctxWithJob.isActive`
    * Code that relies on `.job.isActive` no longer can be called from such entry points safely
* Change the default behaviour -- return `true`. It also "fixes" such patterns as `GlobalScope.isActive` but basically is a breaking change

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

> It also "fixes" such patterns as `GlobalScope.isActive`

`GlobalScope.isActive` is not the same property, and already defaults to `true` when there is no job, so this one is not broken:
https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/master/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/CoroutineScope.kt#L137

This is why I believe it's often more correct to use `CoroutineScope.isActive` rather than `CoroutineContext.isActive`.

> Its only possible replacement is `this.job.isActive`

Why, though? Isn't `this[Job]?.isActive == true` another perfectly valid replacement that doesn't change behaviour? Or is this what you mean by "absence of replacement" - just inlining the property?

I would also be reluctant in suggesting the `.job.isActive` replacement. Very often the authors of those pieces of code just didn't think of the jobless case, and here we would push them towards making their code fail even harder in this case (possibly unknowingly).

> An absence of replacement can be too disturbing as [a lot of code](https://grep.app/search?q=context.isActive&filter%5Blang%5D%5B0%5D=Kotlin) rely on a perfectly fine `ctxWithJob.isActive`

Without better-looking replacement than the body of the current property getter, users could "fix" their code by wrapping the body of their function with `coroutineScope { ... }` and accessing the `CoroutineScope.isActive` property from that scope (which would now have a job). Or just use `yield()` if they want to cancel a non-suspending loop. Or just remove that condition altogether because they didn't need it in the first place (calling other suspend functions with prompt cancellation guarantees already).

Whether we should deprecate `CoroutineContext.isActive` is rather a conceptual matter of whether we want to give meaning to this property when there is no job.

---
TL;DR: I like both the deprecation option and the default change option, but if we deprecate I find the replacement `.job.isActive` harmful. What I like about the change of default is that even though it's technically breaking, it will probably fix more things than break them, and without any change in the ecosystem but version bumps.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/Job.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1‚Üí/*
  2‚Üí * Copyright 2016-2021 JetBrains s.r.o. Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 license.
  3‚Üí */
  4‚Üí
  5‚Üí@file:JvmMultifileClass
  6‚Üí@file:JvmName("JobKt")
  7‚Üí@file:Suppress("DEPRECATION_ERROR", "RedundantUnitReturnType")
  8‚Üí
  9‚Üípackage kotlinx.coroutines
 10‚Üí
 11‚Üíimport kotlinx.coroutines.selects.*
 12‚Üíimport kotlin.coroutines.*
 13‚Üíimport kotlin.jvm.*
 14‚Üí
 15‚Üí// --------------- core job interfaces ---------------
 16‚Üí
 17‚Üí/**
 18‚Üí * A background job. Conceptually, a job is a cancellable thing with a life-cycle that
 19‚Üí * culminates in its completion.
 20‚Üí *
 21‚Üí * Jobs can be arranged into parent-child hierarchies where cancellation
 22‚Üí * of a parent leads to immediate cancellation of all its [children] recursively.
 23‚Üí * Failure of a child with an exception other than [CancellationException] immediately cancels its parent and,
 24‚Üí * consequently, all its other children. This behavior can be customized using [SupervisorJob].
 25‚Üí *
 26‚Üí * The most basic instances of `Job` interface are created like this:
 27‚Üí *
 28‚Üí * * **Coroutine job** is created with [launch][CoroutineScope.launch] coroutine builder.
 29‚Üí *   It runs a specified block of code and completes on completion of this block.
 30‚Üí * * **[CompletableJob]** is created with a `Job()` factory function.
 31‚Üí *   It is completed by calling [CompletableJob.complete].
 32‚Üí *
 33‚Üí * Conceptually, an execution of a job does not produce a result value. Jobs are launched solely for their
 34‚Üí * side effects. See [Deferred] interface for a job that produces a result.
 35‚Üí *
 36‚Üí * ### Job states
 37‚Üí *
 38‚Üí * A job has the following states:
 39‚Üí *
 40‚Üí * | **State**                        | [isActive] | [isCompleted] | [isCancelled] |
 41‚Üí * | -------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------- | ------------- |
 42‚Üí * | _New_ (optional initial state)   | `false`    | `false`       | `false`       |
 43‚Üí * | _Active_ (default initial state) | `true`     | `false`       | `false`       |
 44‚Üí * | _Completing_ (transient state)   | `true`     | `false`       | `false`       |
 45‚Üí * | _Cancelling_ (transient state)   | `false`    | `false`       | `true`        |
 46‚Üí * | _Cancelled_ (final state)        | `false`    | `true`        | `true`        |
 47‚Üí * | _Completed_ (final state)        | `false`    | `true`        | `false`       |
 48‚Üí *
 49‚Üí * Usually, a job is created in the _active_ state (it is created and started). However, coroutine builders
 50‚Üí * that provide an optional `start` parameter create a coroutine in the _new_ state when this parameter is set to
 51‚Üí * [CoroutineStart.LAZY]. Such a job can be made _active_ by invoking [start] or [join].
 52‚Üí *
 53‚Üí * A job is _active_ while the coroutine is working or until [CompletableJob] is completed,
 54‚Üí * or until it fails or cancelled.
 55‚Üí *
 56‚Üí * Failure of an _active_ job with an exception makes it _cancelling_.
 57‚Üí * A job can be cancelled at any time with [cancel] function that forces it to transition to
 58‚Üí * the _cancelling_ state immediately. The job becomes _cancelled_  when it finishes executing its work and
 59‚Üí * all its children complete.
 60‚Üí *
 61‚Üí * Completion of an _active_ coroutine's body or a call to [CompletableJob.complete] transitions the job to
 62‚Üí * the _completing_ state. It waits in the _completing_ state for all its children to complete before
 63‚Üí * transitioning to the _completed_ state.
 64‚Üí * Note that _completing_ state is purely internal to the job. For an outside observer a _completing_ job is still
 65‚Üí * active, while internally it is waiting for its children.
 66‚Üí *
 67‚Üí * ```
 68‚Üí *                                       wait children
 69‚Üí * +-----+ start  +--------+ complete   +-------------+  finish  +-----------+
 70‚Üí * | New | -----> | Active | ---------> | Completing  | -------> | Completed |
 71‚Üí * +-----+        +--------+            +-------------+          +-----------+
 72‚Üí *                  |  cancel / fail       |
 73‚Üí *                  |     +----------------+
 74‚Üí *                  |     |
 75‚Üí *                  V     V
 76‚Üí *              +------------+                           finish  +-----------+
 77‚Üí *              | Cancelling | --------------------------------> | Cancelled |
 78‚Üí *              +------------+                                   +-----------+
 79‚Üí * ```
 80‚Üí *
 81‚Üí * A `Job` instance in the
 82‚Üí * [coroutineContext](https://kotlinlang.org/api/latest/jvm/stdlib/kotlin.coroutines/coroutine-context.html)
 83‚Üí * represents the coroutine itself.
 84‚Üí *
 85‚Üí * ### Cancellation cause
 86‚Üí *
 87‚Üí * A coroutine job is said to _complete exceptionally_ when its body throws an exception;
 88‚Üí * a [CompletableJob] is completed exceptionally by calling [CompletableJob.completeExceptionally].
 89‚Üí * An exceptionally completed job is cancelled and the corresponding exception becomes the _cancellation cause_ of the job.
 90‚Üí *
 91‚Üí * Normal cancellation of a job is distinguished from its failure by the type of this exception that caused its cancellation.
 92‚Üí * A coroutine that threw [CancellationException] is considered to be _cancelled normally_.
 93‚Üí * If a cancellation cause is a different exception type, then the job is considered to have _failed_.
 94‚Üí * When a job has _failed_, then its parent gets cancelled with the exception of the same type,
 95‚Üí * thus ensuring transparency in delegating parts of the job to its children.
 96‚Üí *
 97‚Üí * Note, that [cancel] function on a job only accepts [CancellationException] as a cancellation cause, thus
 98‚Üí * calling [cancel] always results in a normal cancellation of a job, which does not lead to cancellation
 99‚Üí * of its parent. This way, a parent can [cancel] its own children (cancelling all their children recursively, too)
100‚Üí * without cancelling itself.
101‚Üí *
102‚Üí * ### Concurrency and synchronization
103‚Üí *
104‚Üí * All functions on this interface and on all interfaces derived from it are **thread-safe** and can
105‚Üí * be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without external synchronization.
106‚Üí *
107‚Üí * ### Not stable for inheritance
108‚Üí *
109‚Üí * **`Job` interface and all its derived interfaces are not stable for inheritance in 3rd party libraries**,
110‚Üí * as new methods might be added to this interface in the future, but is stable for use.
111‚Üí */
112‚Üípublic interface Job : CoroutineContext.Element {
113‚Üí    /**
114‚Üí     * Key for [Job] instance in the coroutine context.
115‚Üí     */
116‚Üí    public companion object Key : CoroutineContext.Key<Job>
117‚Üí
118‚Üí    // ------------ state query ------------
119‚Üí
120‚Üí    /**
121‚Üí     * Returns the parent of the current job if the parent-child relationship
122‚Üí     * is established or `null` if the job has no parent or was successfully completed.
123‚Üí     *
124‚Üí     * Accesses to this property are not idempotent, the property becomes `null` as soon
125‚Üí     * as the job is transitioned to its final state, whether it is cancelled or completed,
126‚Üí     * and all job children are completed.
127‚Üí     *
128‚Üí     * For a coroutine, its corresponding job completes as soon as the coroutine itself
129‚Üí     * and all its children are complete.
130‚Üí     *
131‚Üí     * @see [Job] state transitions for additional details.
132‚Üí     */
133‚Üí    @ExperimentalCoroutinesApi
134‚Üí    public val parent: Job?
135‚Üí
136‚Üí    /**
137‚Üí     * Returns `true` when this job is active -- it was already started and has not completed nor was cancelled yet.
138‚Üí     * The job that is waiting for its [children] to complete is still considered to be active if it
139‚Üí     * was not cancelled nor failed.
140‚Üí     *
141‚Üí     * See [Job] documentation for more details on job states.
142‚Üí     */
143‚Üí    public val isActive: Boolean
144‚Üí
145‚Üí    /**
146‚Üí     * Returns `true` when this job has completed for any reason. A job that was cancelled or failed
147‚Üí     * and has finished its execution is also considered complete. Job becomes complete only after
148‚Üí     * all its [children] complete.
149‚Üí     *
150‚Üí     * See [Job] documentation for more details on job states.
151‚Üí     */
152‚Üí    public val isCompleted: Boolean
153‚Üí
154‚Üí    /**
155‚Üí     * Returns `true` if this job was cancelled for any reason, either by explicit invocation of [cancel] or
156‚Üí     * because it had failed or its child or parent was cancelled.
157‚Üí     * In the general case, it does not imply that the
158‚Üí     * job has already [completed][isCompleted], because it may still be finishing whatever it was doing and
159‚Üí     * waiting for its [children] to complete.
160‚Üí     *
161‚Üí     * See [Job] documentation for more details on cancellation and failures.
162‚Üí     */
163‚Üí    public val isCancelled: Boolean
164‚Üí
165‚Üí    /**
166‚Üí     * Returns [CancellationException] that signals the completion of this job. This function is
167‚Üí     * used by [cancellable][suspendCancellableCoroutine] suspending functions. They throw exception
168‚Üí     * returned by this function when they suspend in the context of this job and this job becomes _complete_.
169‚Üí     *
170‚Üí     * This function returns the original [cancel] cause of this job if that `cause` was an instance of
171‚Üí     * [CancellationException]. Otherwise (if this job was cancelled with a cause of a different type, or
172‚Üí     * was cancelled without a cause, or had completed normally), an instance of [CancellationException] is
173‚Üí     * returned. The [CancellationException.cause] of the resulting [CancellationException] references
174‚Üí     * the original cancellation cause that was passed to [cancel] function.
175‚Üí     *
176‚Üí     * This function throws [IllegalStateException] when invoked on a job that is still active.
177‚Üí     *
178‚Üí     * @suppress **This an internal API and should not be used from general code.**
179‚Üí     */
180‚Üí    @InternalCoroutinesApi
181‚Üí    public fun getCancellationException(): CancellationException
182‚Üí
183‚Üí    // ------------ state update ------------
184‚Üí
185‚Üí    /**
186‚Üí     * Starts coroutine related to this job (if any) if it was not started yet.
187‚Üí     * The result is `true` if this invocation actually started coroutine or `false`
188‚Üí     * if it was already started or completed.
189‚Üí     */
190‚Üí    public fun start(): Boolean
191‚Üí
192‚Üí
193‚Üí    /**
194‚Üí     * Cancels this job with an optional cancellation [cause].
195‚Üí     * A cause can be used to specify an error message or to provide other details on
196‚Üí     * the cancellation reason for debugging purposes.
197‚Üí     * See [Job] documentation for full explanation of cancellation machinery.
198‚Üí     */
199‚Üí    public fun cancel(cause: CancellationException? = null)
200‚Üí
201‚Üí    /**
202‚Üí     * @suppress This method implements old version of JVM ABI. Use [cancel].
203‚Üí     */
204‚Üí    @Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
205‚Üí    public fun cancel(): Unit = cancel(null)
206‚Üí
207‚Üí    /**
208‚Üí     * @suppress This method has bad semantics when cause is not a [CancellationException]. Use [cancel].
209‚Üí     */
210‚Üí    @Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
211‚Üí    public fun cancel(cause: Throwable? = null): Boolean
212‚Üí
213‚Üí    // ------------ parent-child ------------
214‚Üí
215‚Üí    /**
216‚Üí     * Returns a sequence of this job's children.
217‚Üí     *
218‚Üí     * A job becomes a child of this job when it is constructed with this job in its
219‚Üí     * [CoroutineContext] or using an explicit `parent` parameter.
220‚Üí     *
221‚Üí     * A parent-child relation has the following effect:
222‚Üí     *
223‚Üí     * * Cancellation of parent with [cancel] or its exceptional completion (failure)
224‚Üí     *   immediately cancels all its children.
225‚Üí     * * Parent cannot complete until all its children are complete. Parent waits for all its children to
226‚Üí     *   complete in _completing_ or _cancelling_ state.
227‚Üí     * * Uncaught exception in a child, by default, cancels parent. This applies even to
228‚Üí     *   children created with [async][CoroutineScope.async] and other future-like
229‚Üí     *   coroutine builders, even though their exceptions are caught and are encapsulated in their result.
230‚Üí     *   This default behavior can be overridden with [SupervisorJob].
231‚Üí     */
232‚Üí    public val children: Sequence<Job>
233‚Üí
234‚Üí    /**
235‚Üí     * Attaches child job so that this job becomes its parent and
236‚Üí     * returns a handle that should be used to detach it.
237‚Üí     *
238‚Üí     * A parent-child relation has the following effect:
239‚Üí     * * Cancellation of parent with [cancel] or its exceptional completion (failure)
240‚Üí     *   immediately cancels all its children.
241‚Üí     * * Parent cannot complete until all its children are complete. Parent waits for all its children to
242‚Üí     *   complete in _completing_ or _cancelling_ states.
243‚Üí     *
244‚Üí     * **A child must store the resulting [ChildHandle] and [dispose][DisposableHandle.dispose] the attachment
245‚Üí     * to its parent on its own completion.**
246‚Üí     *
247‚Üí     * Coroutine builders and job factory functions that accept `parent` [CoroutineContext] parameter
248‚Üí     * lookup a [Job] instance in the parent context and use this function to attach themselves as a child.
249‚Üí     * They also store a reference to the resulting [ChildHandle] and dispose a handle when they complete.
250‚Üí     *
251‚Üí     * @suppress This is an internal API. This method is too error prone for public API.
252‚Üí     */
253‚Üí    // ChildJob and ChildHandle are made internal on purpose to further deter 3rd-party impl of Job
254‚Üí    @InternalCoroutinesApi
255‚Üí    public fun attachChild(child: ChildJob): ChildHandle
256‚Üí
257‚Üí    // ------------ state waiting ------------
258‚Üí
259‚Üí    /**
260‚Üí     * Suspends the coroutine until this job is complete. This invocation resumes normally (without exception)
261‚Üí     * when the job is complete for any reason and the [Job] of the invoking coroutine is still [active][isActive].
262‚Üí     * This function also [starts][Job.start] the corresponding coroutine if the [Job] was still in _new_ state.
263‚Üí     *
264‚Üí     * Note that the job becomes complete only when all its children are complete.
265‚Üí     *
266‚Üí     * This suspending function is cancellable and **always** checks for a cancellation of the invoking coroutine's Job.
267‚Üí     * If the [Job] of the invoking coroutine is cancelled or completed when this
268‚Üí     * suspending function is invoked or while it is suspended, this function
269‚Üí     * throws [CancellationException].
270‚Üí     *
271‚Üí     * In particular, it means that a parent coroutine invoking `join` on a child coroutine throws
272‚Üí     * [CancellationException] if the child had failed, since a failure of a child coroutine cancels parent by default,
273‚Üí     * unless the child was launched from within [supervisorScope].
274‚Üí     *
275‚Üí     * This function can be used in [select] invocation with [onJoin] clause.
276‚Üí     * Use [isCompleted] to check for a completion of this job without waiting.
277‚Üí     *
278‚Üí     * There is [cancelAndJoin] function that combines an invocation of [cancel] and `join`.
279‚Üí     */
280‚Üí    public suspend fun join()
281‚Üí
282‚Üí    /**
283‚Üí     * Clause for [select] expression of [join] suspending function that selects when the job is complete.
284‚Üí     * This clause never fails, even if the job completes exceptionally.
285‚Üí     */
286‚Üí    public val onJoin: SelectClause0
287‚Üí
288‚Üí    // ------------ low-level state-notification ------------
289‚Üí
290‚Üí    /**
291‚Üí     * Registers handler that is **synchronously** invoked once on completion of this job.
292‚Üí     * When the job is already complete, then the handler is immediately invoked
293‚Üí     * with the job's exception or cancellation cause or `null`. Otherwise, the handler will be invoked once when this
294‚Üí     * job is complete.
295‚Üí     *
296‚Üí     * The meaning of `cause` that is passed to the handler:
297‚Üí     * * Cause is `null` when the job has completed normally.
298‚Üí     * * Cause is an instance of [CancellationException] when the job was cancelled _normally_.
299‚Üí     *   **It should not be treated as an error**. In particular, it should not be reported to error logs.
300‚Üí     * * Otherwise, the job had _failed_.
301‚Üí     *
302‚Üí     * The resulting [DisposableHandle] can be used to [dispose][DisposableHandle.dispose] the
303‚Üí     * registration of this handler and release its memory if its invocation is no longer needed.
304‚Üí     * There is no need to dispose the handler after completion of this job. The references to
305‚Üí     * all the handlers are released when this job completes.
306‚Üí     *
307‚Üí     * Installed [handler] should not throw any exceptions. If it does, they will get caught,
308‚Üí     * wrapped into [CompletionHandlerException], and rethrown, potentially causing crash of unrelated code.
309‚Üí     *
310‚Üí     * **Note**: Implementation of `CompletionHandler` must be fast, non-blocking, and thread-safe.
311‚Üí     * This handler can be invoked concurrently with the surrounding code.
312‚Üí     * There is no guarantee on the execution context in which the [handler] is invoked.
313‚Üí     */
314‚Üí    public fun invokeOnCompletion(handler: CompletionHandler): DisposableHandle
315‚Üí
316‚Üí    /**
317‚Üí     * Registers handler that is **synchronously** invoked once on cancellation or completion of this job.
318‚Üí     * when the job was already cancelled and is completed its execution, then the handler is immediately invoked
319‚Üí     * with the job's cancellation cause or `null` unless [invokeImmediately] is set to false.
320‚Üí     * Otherwise, handler will be invoked once when this job is cancelled or is complete.
321‚Üí     *
322‚Üí     * The meaning of `cause` that is passed to the handler:
323‚Üí     * * Cause is `null` when the job has completed normally.
324‚Üí     * * Cause is an instance of [CancellationException] when the job was cancelled _normally_.
325‚Üí     *   **It should not be treated as an error**. In particular, it should not be reported to error logs.
326‚Üí     * * Otherwise, the job had _failed_.
327‚Üí     *
328‚Üí     * Invocation of this handler on a transition to a _cancelling_ state
329‚Üí     * is controlled by [onCancelling] boolean parameter.
330‚Üí     * The handler is invoked when the job becomes _cancelling_ if [onCancelling] parameter is set to `true`.
331‚Üí     *
332‚Üí     * The resulting [DisposableHandle] can be used to [dispose][DisposableHandle.dispose] the
333‚Üí     * registration of this handler and release its memory if its invocation is no longer needed.
334‚Üí     * There is no need to dispose the handler after completion of this job. The references to
335‚Üí     * all the handlers are released when this job completes.
336‚Üí     *
337‚Üí     * Installed [handler] should not throw any exceptions. If it does, they will get caught,
338‚Üí     * wrapped into [CompletionHandlerException], and rethrown, potentially causing crash of unrelated code.
339‚Üí     *
340‚Üí     * **Note**: This function is a part of internal machinery that supports parent-child hierarchies
341‚Üí     * and allows for implementation of suspending functions that wait on the Job's state.
342‚Üí     * This function should not be used in general application code.
343‚Üí     * Implementation of `CompletionHandler` must be fast, non-blocking, and thread-safe.
344‚Üí     * This handler can be invoked concurrently with the surrounding code.
345‚Üí     * There is no guarantee on the execution context in which the [handler] is invoked.
346‚Üí     *
347‚Üí     * @param onCancelling when `true`, then the [handler] is invoked as soon as this job transitions to _cancelling_ state;
348‚Üí     *        when `false` then the [handler] is invoked only when it transitions to _completed_ state.
349‚Üí     * @param invokeImmediately when `true` and this job is already in the desired state (depending on [onCancelling]),
350‚Üí     *        then the [handler] is immediately and synchronously invoked and no-op [DisposableHandle] is returned;
351‚Üí     *        when `false` then no-op [DisposableHandle] is returned, but the [handler] is not invoked.
352‚Üí     * @param handler the handler.
353‚Üí     *
354‚Üí     * @suppress **This an internal API and should not be used from general code.**
355‚Üí     */
356‚Üí    @InternalCoroutinesApi
357‚Üí    public fun invokeOnCompletion(
358‚Üí        onCancelling: Boolean = false,
359‚Üí        invokeImmediately: Boolean = true,
360‚Üí        handler: CompletionHandler): DisposableHandle
361‚Üí
362‚Üí    // ------------ unstable internal API ------------
363‚Üí
364‚Üí    /**
365‚Üí     * @suppress **Error**: Operator '+' on two Job objects is meaningless.
366‚Üí     * Job is a coroutine context element and `+` is a set-sum operator for coroutine contexts.
367‚Üí     * The job to the right of `+` just replaces the job the left of `+`.
368‚Üí     */
369‚Üí    @Suppress("DeprecatedCallableAddReplaceWith")
370‚Üí    @Deprecated(message = "Operator '+' on two Job objects is meaningless. " +
371‚Üí        "Job is a coroutine context element and `+` is a set-sum operator for coroutine contexts. " +
372‚Üí        "The job to the right of `+` just replaces the job the left of `+`.",
373‚Üí        level = DeprecationLevel.ERROR)
374‚Üí    public operator fun plus(other: Job): Job = other
375‚Üí}
376‚Üí
377‚Üí/**
378‚Üí * Creates a job object in an active state.
379‚Üí * A failure of any child of this job immediately causes this job to fail, too, and cancels the rest of its children.
380‚Üí *
381‚Üí * To handle children failure independently of each other use [SupervisorJob].
382‚Üí *
383‚Üí * If [parent] job is specified, then this job becomes a child job of its parent and
384‚Üí * is cancelled when its parent fails or is cancelled. All this job's children are cancelled in this case, too.
385‚Üí *
386‚Üí * Conceptually, the resulting job works in the same way as the job created by the `launch { body }` invocation
387‚Üí * (see [launch]), but without any code in the body. It is active until cancelled or completed. Invocation of
388‚Üí * [CompletableJob.complete] or [CompletableJob.completeExceptionally] corresponds to the successful or
389‚Üí * failed completion of the body of the coroutine.
390‚Üí *
391‚Üí * @param parent an optional parent job.
392‚Üí */
393‚Üí@Suppress("FunctionName")
394‚Üípublic fun Job(parent: Job? = null): CompletableJob = JobImpl(parent)
395‚Üí
396‚Üí/** @suppress Binary compatibility only */
397‚Üí@Suppress("FunctionName")
398‚Üí@Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
399‚Üí@JvmName("Job")
400‚Üípublic fun Job0(parent: Job? = null): Job = Job(parent)
401‚Üí
402‚Üí/**
403‚Üí * A handle to an allocated object that can be disposed to make it eligible for garbage collection.
404‚Üí */
405‚Üípublic fun interface DisposableHandle {
406‚Üí    /**
407‚Üí     * Disposes the corresponding object, making it eligible for garbage collection.
408‚Üí     * Repeated invocation of this function has no effect.
409‚Üí     */
410‚Üí    public fun dispose()
411‚Üí}
412‚Üí
413‚Üí// -------------------- Parent-child communication --------------------
414‚Üí
415‚Üí/**
416‚Üí * A reference that parent receives from its child so that it can report its cancellation.
417‚Üí *
418‚Üí * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
419‚Üí */
420‚Üí@InternalCoroutinesApi
421‚Üí@Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.ERROR, message = "This is internal API and may be removed in the future releases")
422‚Üípublic interface ChildJob : Job {
423‚Üí    /**
424‚Üí     * Parent is cancelling its child by invoking this method.
425‚Üí     * Child finds the cancellation cause using [ParentJob.getChildJobCancellationCause].
426‚Üí     * This method does nothing is the child is already being cancelled.
427‚Üí     *
428‚Üí     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
429‚Üí     */
430‚Üí    @InternalCoroutinesApi
431‚Üí    public fun parentCancelled(parentJob: ParentJob)
432‚Üí}
433‚Üí
434‚Üí/**
435‚Üí * A reference that child receives from its parent when it is being cancelled by the parent.
436‚Üí *
437‚Üí * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
438‚Üí */
439‚Üí@InternalCoroutinesApi
440‚Üí@Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.ERROR, message = "This is internal API and may be removed in the future releases")
441‚Üípublic interface ParentJob : Job {
442‚Üí    /**
443‚Üí     * Child job is using this method to learn its cancellation cause when the parent cancels it with [ChildJob.parentCancelled].
444‚Üí     * This method is invoked only if the child was not already being cancelled.
445‚Üí     *
446‚Üí     * Note that [CancellationException] is the method's return type: if child is cancelled by its parent,
447‚Üí     * then the original exception is **already** handled by either the parent or the original source of failure.
448‚Üí     *
449‚Üí     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
450‚Üí     */
451‚Üí    @InternalCoroutinesApi
452‚Üí    public fun getChildJobCancellationCause(): CancellationException
453‚Üí}
454‚Üí
455‚Üí/**
456‚Üí * A handle that child keep onto its parent so that it is able to report its cancellation.
457‚Üí *
458‚Üí * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
459‚Üí */
460‚Üí@InternalCoroutinesApi
461‚Üí@Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.ERROR, message = "This is internal API and may be removed in the future releases")
462‚Üípublic interface ChildHandle : DisposableHandle {
463‚Üí
464‚Üí    /**
465‚Üí     * Returns the parent of the current parent-child relationship.
466‚Üí     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
467‚Üí     */
468‚Üí    @InternalCoroutinesApi
469‚Üí    public val parent: Job?
470‚Üí
471‚Üí    /**
472‚Üí     * Child is cancelling its parent by invoking this method.
473‚Üí     * This method is invoked by the child twice. The first time child report its root cause as soon as possible,
474‚Üí     * so that all its siblings and the parent can start cancelling their work asap. The second time
475‚Üí     * child invokes this method when it had aggregated and determined its final cancellation cause.
476‚Üí     *
477‚Üí     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
478‚Üí     */
479‚Üí    @InternalCoroutinesApi
480‚Üí    public fun childCancelled(cause: Throwable): Boolean
481‚Üí}
482‚Üí
483‚Üí// -------------------- Job extensions --------------------
484‚Üí
485‚Üí/**
486‚Üí * Disposes a specified [handle] when this job is complete.
487‚Üí *
488‚Üí * This is a shortcut for the following code with slightly more efficient implementation (one fewer object created).
489‚Üí * ```
490‚Üí * invokeOnCompletion { handle.dispose() }
491‚Üí * ```
492‚Üí */
493‚Üíinternal fun Job.disposeOnCompletion(handle: DisposableHandle): DisposableHandle =
494‚Üí    invokeOnCompletion(handler = DisposeOnCompletion(handle).asHandler)
495‚Üí
496‚Üí/**
497‚Üí * Cancels the job and suspends the invoking coroutine until the cancelled job is complete.
498‚Üí *
499‚Üí * This suspending function is cancellable and **always** checks for a cancellation of the invoking coroutine's Job.
500‚Üí * If the [Job] of the invoking coroutine is cancelled or completed when this
501‚Üí * suspending function is invoked or while it is suspended, this function
502‚Üí * throws [CancellationException].
503‚Üí *
504‚Üí * In particular, it means that a parent coroutine invoking `cancelAndJoin` on a child coroutine throws
505‚Üí * [CancellationException] if the child had failed, since a failure of a child coroutine cancels parent by default,
506‚Üí * unless the child was launched from within [supervisorScope].
507‚Üí *
508‚Üí * This is a shortcut for the invocation of [cancel][Job.cancel] followed by [join][Job.join].
509‚Üí */
510‚Üípublic suspend fun Job.cancelAndJoin() {
511‚Üí    cancel()
512‚Üí    return join()
513‚Üí}
514‚Üí
515‚Üí/**
516‚Üí * Cancels all [children][Job.children] jobs of this coroutine using [Job.cancel] for all of them
517‚Üí * with an optional cancellation [cause].
518‚Üí * Unlike [Job.cancel] on this job as a whole, the state of this job itself is not affected.
519‚Üí */
520‚Üípublic fun Job.cancelChildren(cause: CancellationException? = null) {
521‚Üí    children.forEach { it.cancel(cause) }
522‚Üí}
523‚Üí
524‚Üí/**
525‚Üí * @suppress This method implements old version of JVM ABI. Use [cancel].
526‚Üí */
527‚Üí@Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
528‚Üípublic fun Job.cancelChildren(): Unit = cancelChildren(null)
529‚Üí
530‚Üí/**
531‚Üí * @suppress This method has bad semantics when cause is not a [CancellationException]. Use [Job.cancelChildren].
532‚Üí */
533‚Üí@Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
534‚Üípublic fun Job.cancelChildren(cause: Throwable? = null) {
535‚Üí    children.forEach { (it as? JobSupport)?.cancelInternal(cause.orCancellation(this)) }
536‚Üí}
537‚Üí
538‚Üí// -------------------- CoroutineContext extensions --------------------
539‚Üí
540‚Üí/**
541‚Üí * Returns `true` when the [Job] of the coroutine in this context is still active
542‚Üí * (has not completed and was not cancelled yet).
543‚Üí *
544‚Üí * Check this property in long-running computation loops to support cancellation
545‚Üí * when [CoroutineScope.isActive] is not available:
546‚Üí *
547‚Üí * ```
548‚Üí * while (coroutineContext.isActive) {
549‚Üí *     // do some computation
550‚Üí * }
551‚Üí * ```
552‚Üí *
553‚Üí * The `coroutineContext.isActive` expression is a shortcut for `coroutineContext[Job]?.isActive == true`.
554‚Üí * See [Job.isActive].
555‚Üí */
556‚Üípublic val CoroutineContext.isActive: Boolean
557‚Üí    get() = this[Job]?.isActive == true
558‚Üí
559‚Üí/**
560‚Üí * Cancels [Job] of this context with an optional cancellation cause.
561‚Üí * See [Job.cancel] for details.
562‚Üí */
563‚Üípublic fun CoroutineContext.cancel(cause: CancellationException? = null) {
564‚Üí    this[Job]?.cancel(cause)
565‚Üí}
566‚Üí
567‚Üí/**
568‚Üí * @suppress This method implements old version of JVM ABI. Use [CoroutineContext.cancel].
569‚Üí */
570‚Üí@Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
571‚Üípublic fun CoroutineContext.cancel(): Unit = cancel(null)
572‚Üí
573‚Üí/**
574‚Üí * Ensures that current job is [active][Job.isActive].
575‚Üí * If the job is no longer active, throws [CancellationException].
576‚Üí * If the job was cancelled, thrown exception contains the original cancellation cause.
577‚Üí *
578‚Üí * This method is a drop-in replacement for the following code, but with more precise exception:
579‚Üí * ```
580‚Üí * if (!job.isActive) {
581‚Üí *     throw CancellationException()
582‚Üí * }
583‚Üí * ```
584‚Üí */
585‚Üípublic fun Job.ensureActive(): Unit {
586‚Üí    if (!isActive) throw getCancellationException()
587‚Üí}
588‚Üí
589‚Üí/**
590‚Üí * Ensures that job in the current context is [active][Job.isActive].
591‚Üí *
592‚Üí * If the job is no longer active, throws [CancellationException].
593‚Üí * If the job was cancelled, thrown exception contains the original cancellation cause.
594‚Üí * This function does not do anything if there is no [Job] in the context, since such a coroutine cannot be cancelled.
595‚Üí *
596‚Üí * This method is a drop-in replacement for the following code, but with more precise exception:
597‚Üí * ```
598‚Üí * if (!isActive) {
599‚Üí *     throw CancellationException()
600‚Üí * }
601‚Üí * ```
602‚Üí */
603‚Üípublic fun CoroutineContext.ensureActive() {
604‚Üí    get(Job)?.ensureActive()
605‚Üí}
606‚Üí
607‚Üí/**
608‚Üí * Cancels current job, including all its children with a specified diagnostic error [message].
609‚Üí * A [cause] can be specified to provide additional details on a cancellation reason for debugging purposes.
610‚Üí */
611‚Üípublic fun Job.cancel(message: String, cause: Throwable? = null): Unit = cancel(CancellationException(message, cause))
612‚Üí
613‚Üí/**
614‚Üí * @suppress This method has bad semantics when cause is not a [CancellationException]. Use [CoroutineContext.cancel].
615‚Üí */
616‚Üí@Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
617‚Üípublic fun CoroutineContext.cancel(cause: Throwable? = null): Boolean {
618‚Üí    val job = this[Job] as? JobSupport ?: return false
619‚Üí    job.cancelInternal(cause.orCancellation(job))
620‚Üí    return true
621‚Üí}
622‚Üí
623‚Üí/**
624‚Üí * Cancels all children of the [Job] in this context, without touching the state of this job itself
625‚Üí * with an optional cancellation cause. See [Job.cancel].
626‚Üí * It does not do anything if there is no job in the context or it has no children.
627‚Üí */
628‚Üípublic fun CoroutineContext.cancelChildren(cause: CancellationException? = null) {
629‚Üí    this[Job]?.children?.forEach { it.cancel(cause) }
630‚Üí}
631‚Üí
632‚Üí/**
633‚Üí * @suppress This method implements old version of JVM ABI. Use [CoroutineContext.cancelChildren].
634‚Üí */
635‚Üí@Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
636‚Üípublic fun CoroutineContext.cancelChildren(): Unit = cancelChildren(null)
637‚Üí
638‚Üí/**
639‚Üí * Retrieves the current [Job] instance from the given [CoroutineContext] or
640‚Üí * throws [IllegalStateException] if no job is present in the context.
641‚Üí *
642‚Üí * This method is a short-cut for `coroutineContext[Job]!!` and should be used only when it is known in advance that
643‚Üí * the context does have instance of the job in it.
644‚Üí */
645‚Üípublic val CoroutineContext.job: Job get() = get(Job) ?: error("Current context doesn't contain Job in it: $this")
646‚Üí
647‚Üí/**
648‚Üí * @suppress This method has bad semantics when cause is not a [CancellationException]. Use [CoroutineContext.cancelChildren].
649‚Üí */
650‚Üí@Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
651‚Üípublic fun CoroutineContext.cancelChildren(cause: Throwable? = null) {
652‚Üí    val job = this[Job] ?: return
653‚Üí    job.children.forEach { (it as? JobSupport)?.cancelInternal(cause.orCancellation(job)) }
654‚Üí}
655‚Üí
656‚Üíprivate fun Throwable?.orCancellation(job: Job): Throwable = this ?: JobCancellationException("Job was cancelled", null, job)
657‚Üí
658‚Üí/**
659‚Üí * No-op implementation of [DisposableHandle].
660‚Üí * @suppress **This an internal API and should not be used from general code.**
661‚Üí */
662‚Üí@InternalCoroutinesApi
663‚Üípublic object NonDisposableHandle : DisposableHandle, ChildHandle {
664‚Üí
665‚Üí    override val parent: Job? get() = null
666‚Üí
667‚Üí    /**
668‚Üí     * Does not do anything.
669‚Üí     * @suppress
670‚Üí     */
671‚Üí    override fun dispose() {}
672‚Üí
673‚Üí    /**
674‚Üí     * Returns `false`.
675‚Üí     * @suppress
676‚Üí     */
677‚Üí    override fun childCancelled(cause: Throwable): Boolean = false
678‚Üí
679‚Üí    /**
680‚Üí     * Returns "NonDisposableHandle" string.
681‚Üí     * @suppress
682‚Üí     */
683‚Üí    override fun toString(): String = "NonDisposableHandle"
684‚Üí}

 14‚Üí/**
 15‚Üí * Defines a scope for new coroutines. Every **coroutine builder** (like [launch], [async], etc.)
 16‚Üí * is an extension on [CoroutineScope] and inherits its [coroutineContext][CoroutineScope.coroutineContext]
 17‚Üí * to automatically propagate all its elements and cancellation.
 18‚Üí *
 19‚Üí * The best ways to obtain a standalone instance of the scope are [CoroutineScope()] and [MainScope()] factory functions,
 20‚Üí * taking care to cancel these coroutine scopes when they are no longer needed (see section on custom usage below for
 21‚Üí * explanation and example).
 22‚Üí *
 23‚Üí * Additional context elements can be appended to the scope using the [plus][CoroutineScope.plus] operator.
 24‚Üí *
 25‚Üí * ### Convention for structured concurrency
 26‚Üí *
 27‚Üí * Manual implementation of this interface is not recommended, implementation by delegation should be preferred instead.
 28‚Üí * By convention, the [context of a scope][CoroutineScope.coroutineContext] should contain an instance of a
 29‚Üí * [job][Job] to enforce the discipline of **structured concurrency** with propagation of cancellation.
 30‚Üí *
 31‚Üí * Every coroutine builder (like [launch], [async], and others)
 32‚Üí * and every scoping function (like [coroutineScope] and [withContext]) provides _its own_ scope
 33‚Üí * with its own [Job] instance into the inner block of code it runs.
 34‚Üí * By convention, they all wait for all the coroutines inside their block to complete before completing themselves,
 35‚Üí * thus enforcing the structured concurrency. See [Job] documentation for more details.
 36‚Üí *
 37‚Üí * ### Android usage
 38‚Üí *
 39‚Üí * Android has first-party support for coroutine scope in all entities with the lifecycle.
 40‚Üí * See [the corresponding documentation](https://developer.android.com/topic/libraries/architecture/coroutines#lifecyclescope).
 41‚Üí *
 42‚Üí * ### Custom usage
 43‚Üí *
 44‚Üí * `CoroutineScope` should be declared as a property on entities with a well-defined lifecycle that are
 45‚Üí * responsible for launching child coroutines. The corresponding instance of `CoroutineScope` shall be created
 46‚Üí * with either `CoroutineScope()` or `MainScope()`:
 47‚Üí *
 48‚Üí * * `CoroutineScope()` uses the [context][CoroutineContext] provided to it as a parameter for its coroutines 
 49‚Üí *   and adds a [Job] if one is not provided as part of the context.
 50‚Üí * * `MainScope()` uses [Dispatchers.Main] for its coroutines and has a [SupervisorJob].
 51‚Üí *
 52‚Üí * **The key part of custom usage of `CoroutineScope` is cancelling it at the end of the lifecycle.**
 53‚Üí * The [CoroutineScope.cancel] extension function shall be used when the entity that was launching coroutines
 54‚Üí * is no longer needed. It cancels all the coroutines that might still be running on behalf of it.
 55‚Üí *
 56‚Üí * For example:
 57‚Üí *
 58‚Üí * ```
 59‚Üí * class MyUIClass {
 60‚Üí *     val scope = MainScope() // the scope of MyUIClass, uses Dispatchers.Main
 61‚Üí *
 62‚Üí *     fun destroy() { // destroys an instance of MyUIClass
 63‚Üí *         scope.cancel() // cancels all coroutines launched in this scope
 64‚Üí *         // ... do the rest of cleanup here ...
 65‚Üí *     }
 66‚Üí *
 67‚Üí *     /*
 68‚Üí *      * Note: if this instance is destroyed or any of the launched coroutines
 69‚Üí *      * in this method throws an exception, then all nested coroutines are cancelled.
 70‚Üí *      */
 71‚Üí *     fun showSomeData() = scope.launch { // launched in the main thread
 72‚Üí *        // ... here we can use suspending functions or coroutine builders with other dispatchers
 73‚Üí *        draw(data) // draw in the main thread
 74‚Üí *     }
 75‚Üí * }
 76‚Üí * ```
 77‚Üí */
 78‚Üípublic interface CoroutineScope {
 79‚Üí    /**
 80‚Üí     * The context of this scope.
 81‚Üí     * Context is encapsulated by the scope and used for implementation of coroutine builders that are extensions on the scope.
 82‚Üí     * Accessing this property in general code is not recommended for any purposes except accessing the [Job] instance for advanced usages.
 83‚Üí     *
 84‚Üí     * By convention, should contain an instance of a [job][Job] to enforce structured concurrency.
 85‚Üí     */
 86‚Üí    public val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext
 87‚Üí}
 88‚Üí
 89‚Üí/**
 90‚Üí * Adds the specified coroutine context to this scope, overriding existing elements in the current
 91‚Üí * scope's context with the corresponding keys.
 92‚Üí *
 93‚Üí * This is a shorthand for `CoroutineScope(thisScope + context)`.
 94‚Üí */
 95‚Üípublic operator fun CoroutineScope.plus(context: CoroutineContext): CoroutineScope =
 96‚Üí    ContextScope(coroutineContext + context)
 97‚Üí
 98‚Üí/**
 99‚Üí * Creates the main [CoroutineScope] for UI components.
100‚Üí *
101‚Üí * Example of use:
102‚Üí * ```
103‚Üí * class MyAndroidActivity {
104‚Üí *     private val scope = MainScope()
105‚Üí *
106‚Üí *     override fun onDestroy() {
107‚Üí *         super.onDestroy()
108‚Üí *         scope.cancel()
109‚Üí *     }
110‚Üí * }
111‚Üí * ```
112‚Üí *
113‚Üí * The resulting scope has [SupervisorJob] and [Dispatchers.Main] context elements.
114‚Üí * If you want to append additional elements to the main scope, use [CoroutineScope.plus] operator:
115‚Üí * `val scope = MainScope() + CoroutineName("MyActivity")`.
116‚Üí */
117‚Üí@Suppress("FunctionName")
118‚Üípublic fun MainScope(): CoroutineScope = ContextScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.Main)
119‚Üí
120‚Üí/**
121‚Üí * Returns `true` when the current [Job] is still active (has not completed and was not cancelled yet).
122‚Üí *
123‚Üí * Check this property in long-running computation loops to support cancellation:
124‚Üí * ```
125‚Üí * while (isActive) {
126‚Üí *     // do some computation
127‚Üí * }
128‚Üí * ```
129‚Üí *
130‚Üí * This property is a shortcut for `coroutineContext.isActive` in the scope when
131‚Üí * [CoroutineScope] is available.
132‚Üí * See [coroutineContext][kotlin.coroutines.coroutineContext],
133‚Üí * [isActive][kotlinx.coroutines.isActive] and [Job.isActive].
134‚Üí */
135‚Üí@Suppress("EXTENSION_SHADOWED_BY_MEMBER")
136‚Üípublic val CoroutineScope.isActive: Boolean
137‚Üí    get() = coroutineContext[Job]?.isActive ?: true
138‚Üí
139‚Üí/**
140‚Üí * A global [CoroutineScope] not bound to any job.
141‚Üí * Global scope is used to launch top-level coroutines which are operating on the whole application lifetime
142‚Üí * and are not cancelled prematurely.
143‚Üí *
144‚Üí * Active coroutines launched in `GlobalScope` do not keep the process alive. They are like daemon threads.
145‚Üí *
146‚Üí * This is a **delicate** API. It is easy to accidentally create resource or memory leaks when
147‚Üí * `GlobalScope` is used. A coroutine launched in `GlobalScope` is not subject to the principle of structured
148‚Üí * concurrency, so if it hangs or gets delayed due to a problem (e.g. due to a slow network), it will stay working
149‚Üí * and consuming resources. For example, consider the following code:
150‚Üí *
151‚Üí * ```
152‚Üí * fun loadConfiguration() {
153‚Üí *     GlobalScope.launch {
154‚Üí *         val config = fetchConfigFromServer() // network request
155‚Üí *         updateConfiguration(config)
156‚Üí *     }
157‚Üí * }
158‚Üí * ```
159‚Üí *
160‚Üí * A call to `loadConfiguration` creates a coroutine in the `GlobalScope` that works in background without any
161‚Üí * provision to cancel it or to wait for its completion. If a network is slow, it keeps waiting in background,
162‚Üí * consuming resources. Repeated calls to `loadConfiguration` will consume more and more resources.
163‚Üí *
164‚Üí * ### Possible replacements
165‚Üí *
166‚Üí * In many cases uses of `GlobalScope` should be removed, marking the containing operation with `suspend`, for example:
167‚Üí *
168‚Üí * ```
169‚Üí * suspend fun loadConfiguration() {
170‚Üí *     val config = fetchConfigFromServer() // network request
171‚Üí *     updateConfiguration(config)
172‚Üí * }
173‚Üí * ```
174‚Üí *
175‚Üí * In cases when `GlobalScope.launch` was used to launch multiple concurrent operations, the corresponding
176‚Üí * operations shall be grouped with [coroutineScope] instead:
177‚Üí *
178‚Üí * ```
179‚Üí * // concurrently load configuration and data
180‚Üí * suspend fun loadConfigurationAndData() {
181‚Üí *     coroutineScope {
182‚Üí *         launch { loadConfiguration() }
183‚Üí *         launch { loadData() }
184‚Üí *     }
185‚Üí * }
186‚Üí * ```
187‚Üí *
188‚Üí * In top-level code, when launching a concurrent operation from a non-suspending context, an appropriately
189‚Üí * confined instance of [CoroutineScope] shall be used instead of a `GlobalScope`. See docs on [CoroutineScope] for
190‚Üí * details.
191‚Üí *
192‚Üí * ### GlobalScope vs custom scope
193‚Üí *
194‚Üí * Do not replace `GlobalScope.launch { ... }` with `CoroutineScope().launch { ... }` constructor function call.
195‚Üí * The latter has the same pitfalls as `GlobalScope`. See [CoroutineScope] documentation on the intended usage of
196‚Üí * `CoroutineScope()` constructor function.
197‚Üí *
198‚Üí * ### Legitimate use-cases
199‚Üí *
200‚Üí * There are limited circumstances under which `GlobalScope` can be legitimately and safely used, such as top-level background
201‚Üí * processes that must stay active for the whole duration of the application's lifetime. Because of that, any use
202‚Üí * of `GlobalScope` requires an explicit opt-in with `@OptIn(DelicateCoroutinesApi::class)`, like this:
203‚Üí *
204‚Üí * ```
205‚Üí * // A global coroutine to log statistics every second, must be always active
206‚Üí * @OptIn(DelicateCoroutinesApi::class)
207‚Üí * val globalScopeReporter = GlobalScope.launch {
208‚Üí *     while (true) {
209‚Üí *         delay(1000)
210‚Üí *         logStatistics()
211‚Üí *     }
212‚Üí * }
213‚Üí * ```
214‚Üí */
215‚Üí@DelicateCoroutinesApi
216‚Üípublic object GlobalScope : CoroutineScope {
217‚Üí    /**
218‚Üí     * Returns [EmptyCoroutineContext].
219‚Üí     */
220‚Üí    override val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext
221‚Üí        get() = EmptyCoroutineContext
222‚Üí}
223‚Üí
224‚Üí/**
225‚Üí * Creates a [CoroutineScope] and calls the specified suspend block with this scope.
226‚Üí * The provided scope inherits its [coroutineContext][CoroutineScope.coroutineContext] from the outer scope, but overrides
227‚Üí * the context's [Job].
228‚Üí *
229‚Üí * This function is designed for _parallel decomposition_ of work. When any child coroutine in this scope fails,
230‚Üí * this scope fails and all the rest of the children are cancelled (for a different behavior see [supervisorScope]).
231‚Üí * This function returns as soon as the given block and all its children coroutines are completed.
232‚Üí * A usage example of a scope looks like this:
233‚Üí *
234‚Üí * ```
235‚Üí * suspend fun showSomeData() = coroutineScope {
236‚Üí *     val data = async(Dispatchers.IO) { // <- extension on current scope
237‚Üí *      ... load some UI data for the Main thread ...
238‚Üí *     }
239‚Üí *
240‚Üí *     withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
241‚Üí *         doSomeWork()
242‚Üí *         val result = data.await()
243‚Üí *         display(result)
244‚Üí *     }
245‚Üí * }
246‚Üí * ```
247‚Üí *
248‚Üí * The scope in this example has the following semantics:
249‚Üí * 1) `showSomeData` returns as soon as the data is loaded and displayed in the UI.
250‚Üí * 2) If `doSomeWork` throws an exception, then the `async` task is cancelled and `showSomeData` rethrows that exception.
251‚Üí * 3) If the outer scope of `showSomeData` is cancelled, both started `async` and `withContext` blocks are cancelled.
252‚Üí * 4) If the `async` block fails, `withContext` will be cancelled.
253‚Üí *
254‚Üí * The method may throw a [CancellationException] if the current job was cancelled externally
255‚Üí * or may throw a corresponding unhandled [Throwable] if there is any unhandled exception in this scope
256‚Üí * (for example, from a crashed coroutine that was started with [launch][CoroutineScope.launch] in this scope).
257‚Üí */
258‚Üípublic suspend fun <R> coroutineScope(block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> R): R {
259‚Üí    contract {
260‚Üí        callsInPlace(block, InvocationKind.EXACTLY_ONCE)
261‚Üí    }
262‚Üí    return suspendCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn { uCont ->
263‚Üí        val coroutine = ScopeCoroutine(uCont.context, uCont)
264‚Üí        coroutine.startUndispatchedOrReturn(coroutine, block)
265‚Üí    }
266‚Üí}
267‚Üí
268‚Üí/**
269‚Üí * Creates a [CoroutineScope] that wraps the given coroutine [context].
270‚Üí *
271‚Üí * If the given [context] does not contain a [Job] element, then a default `Job()` is created.
272‚Üí * This way, failure of any child coroutine in this scope or [cancellation][CoroutineScope.cancel] of the scope itself
273‚Üí * cancels all the scope's children, just like inside [coroutineScope] block.
274‚Üí */
275‚Üí@Suppress("FunctionName")
276‚Üípublic fun CoroutineScope(context: CoroutineContext): CoroutineScope =
277‚Üí    ContextScope(if (context[Job] != null) context else context + Job())
278‚Üí
279‚Üí/**
280‚Üí * Cancels this scope, including its job and all its children with an optional cancellation [cause].
281‚Üí * A cause can be used to specify an error message or to provide other details on
282‚Üí * a cancellation reason for debugging purposes.
283‚Üí * Throws [IllegalStateException] if the scope does not have a job in it.
284‚Üí */
285‚Üípublic fun CoroutineScope.cancel(cause: CancellationException? = null) {
286‚Üí    val job = coroutineContext[Job] ?: error("Scope cannot be cancelled because it does not have a job: $this")
287‚Üí    job.cancel(cause)
288‚Üí}
289‚Üí
290‚Üí/**
291‚Üí * Cancels this scope, including its job and all its children with a specified diagnostic error [message].
292‚Üí * A [cause] can be specified to provide additional details on a cancellation reason for debugging purposes.
293‚Üí * Throws [IllegalStateException] if the scope does not have a job in it.
294‚Üí */
295‚Üípublic fun CoroutineScope.cancel(message: String, cause: Throwable? = null): Unit = cancel(CancellationException(message, cause))
296‚Üí
297‚Üí/**
298‚Üí * Ensures that current scope is [active][CoroutineScope.isActive].
299‚Üí *
300‚Üí * If the job is no longer active, throws [CancellationException].
301‚Üí * If the job was cancelled, thrown exception contains the original cancellation cause.
302‚Üí * This function does not do anything if there is no [Job] in the scope's [coroutineContext][CoroutineScope.coroutineContext].
303‚Üí *
304‚Üí * This method is a drop-in replacement for the following code, but with more precise exception:
305‚Üí * ```
306‚Üí * if (!isActive) {
307‚Üí *     throw CancellationException()
308‚Üí * }
309‚Üí * ```
310‚Üí *
311‚Üí * @see CoroutineContext.ensureActive
312‚Üí */
313‚Üípublic fun CoroutineScope.ensureActive(): Unit = coroutineContext.ensureActive()
314‚Üí
315‚Üí
316‚Üí/**
317‚Üí * Returns the current [CoroutineContext] retrieved by using [kotlin.coroutines.coroutineContext].
318‚Üí * This function is an alias to avoid name clash with [CoroutineScope.coroutineContext] in a receiver position:
319‚Üí *
320‚Üí * ```
321‚Üí * launch { // this: CoroutineScope
322‚Üí *     val flow = flow<Unit> {
323‚Üí *         coroutineContext // Resolves into the context of outer launch, which is incorrect, see KT-38033
324‚Üí *         currentCoroutineContext() // Retrieves actual context where the flow is collected
325‚Üí *     }
326‚Üí * }
327‚Üí * ```
328‚Üí */
329‚Üípublic suspend inline fun currentCoroutineContext(): CoroutineContext = coroutineContext

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue well. Let me compare the two implementations:

1. `CoroutineContext.isActive` (in Job.kt, line 556-557):
```kotlin
public val CoroutineContext.isActive: Boolean
    get() = this[Job]?.isActive == true
```
This returns `false` when there's no Job in the context.

2. `CoroutineScope.isActive` (in CoroutineScope.kt, line 136-137):
```kotlin
public val CoroutineScope.isActive: Boolean
    get() = coroutineContext[Job]?.isActive ?: true
```
This returns `true` when there's no Job in the context.

The issue is that `CoroutineContext.isActive` returns `false` when there's no Job, which is inconsistent with the contract (that a job "has not been completed and was not cancelled yet") and can be error-prone when used with entry points like Ktor and `suspend fun main`.

According to the issue discussion, the preferred solution seems to be changing the default behavior to return `true` when there's no Job, which aligns with how `CoroutineScope.isActive` works.

Let me also look for existing tests to understand the expected behavior better.

 22‚Üí/**
 23‚Üí * Launches a new coroutine without blocking the current thread and returns a reference to the coroutine as a [Job].
 24‚Üí * The coroutine is cancelled when the resulting job is [cancelled][Job.cancel].
 25‚Üí *
 26‚Üí * The coroutine context is inherited from a [CoroutineScope]. Additional context elements can be specified with [context] argument.
 27‚Üí * If the context does not have any dispatcher nor any other [ContinuationInterceptor], then [Dispatchers.Default] is used.
 28‚Üí * The parent job is inherited from a [CoroutineScope] as well, but it can also be overridden
 29‚Üí * with a corresponding [context] element.
 30‚Üí *
 31‚Üí * By default, the coroutine is immediately scheduled for execution.
 32‚Üí * Other start options can be specified via `start` parameter. See [CoroutineStart] for details.
 33‚Üí * An optional [start] parameter can be set to [CoroutineStart.LAZY] to start coroutine _lazily_. In this case,
 34‚Üí * the coroutine [Job] is created in _new_ state. It can be explicitly started with [start][Job.start] function
 35‚Üí * and will be started implicitly on the first invocation of [join][Job.join].
 36‚Üí *
 37‚Üí * Uncaught exceptions in this coroutine cancel the parent job in the context by default
 38‚Üí * (unless [CoroutineExceptionHandler] is explicitly specified), which means that when `launch` is used with
 39‚Üí * the context of another coroutine, then any uncaught exception leads to the cancellation of the parent coroutine.
 40‚Üí *
 41‚Üí * See [newCoroutineContext] for a description of debugging facilities that are available for a newly created coroutine.

 44‚Üí * @param start coroutine start option. The default value is [CoroutineStart.DEFAULT].
 45‚Üí * @param block the coroutine code which will be invoked in the context of the provided scope.
 46‚Üí **/

 48‚Üí    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
 49‚Üí    start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT,
 50‚Üí    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> Unit
 51‚Üí): Job {
 52‚Üí    val newContext = newCoroutineContext(context)
 53‚Üí    val coroutine = if (start.isLazy)
 54‚Üí        LazyStandaloneCoroutine(newContext, block) else
 55‚Üí        StandaloneCoroutine(newContext, active = true)

151‚Üí        val oldContext = uCont.context
152‚Üí        // Copy CopyableThreadContextElement if necessary
153‚Üí        val newContext = oldContext.newCoroutineContext(context)
154‚Üí        // always check for cancellation of new context
155‚Üí        newContext.ensureActive()
156‚Üí        // FAST PATH #1 -- new context is the same as the old one
157‚Üí        if (newContext === oldContext) {

162‚Üí        // `equals` is used by design (see equals implementation is wrapper context like ExecutorCoroutineDispatcher)
163‚Üí        if (newContext[ContinuationInterceptor] == oldContext[ContinuationInterceptor]) {

171‚Üí        val coroutine = DispatchedCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
172‚Üí        block.startCoroutineCancellable(coroutine, coroutine)

212‚Üí    context: CoroutineContext,
213‚Üí    uCont: Continuation<T>
214‚Üí) : ScopeCoroutine<T>
215‚Üí
216‚Üíprivate const val UNDECIDED = 0
217‚Üíprivate const val SUSPENDED = 1
218‚Üíprivate const val RESUMED = 2
219‚Üí
220‚Üí// Used by withContext when context dispatcher changes
221‚Üíinternal class DispatchedCoroutine<in T>(
222‚Üí    context: CoroutineContext,
223‚Üí    uCont: Continuation<T>
224‚Üí) : ScopeCoroutine<T>(context, uCont) {
225‚Üí    // this is copy-and-paste of a decision state machine inside AbstractionContinuation
226‚Üí    // todo: we may some-how abstract it via inline class
227‚Üí    private val _decision = atomic(UNDECIDED)
228‚Üí
229‚Üí    private fun trySuspend(): Boolean {
230‚Üí        _decision.loop { decision ->
231‚Üí            when (decision) {
232‚Üí                UNDECIDED -> if (this._decision.compareAndSet(UNDECIDED, SUSPENDED)) return true
233‚Üí                RESUMED -> return false
234‚Üí                else -> error("Already suspended")
235‚Üí            }
236‚Üí        }
237‚Üí    }
238‚Üí
239‚Üí    private fun tryResume(): Boolean {
240‚Üí        _decision.loop { decision ->
241‚Üí            when (decision) {
242‚Üí                UNDECIDED -> if (this._decision.compareAndSet(UNDECIDED, RESUMED)) return true
243‚Üí                SUSPENDED -> return false
244‚Üí                else -> error("Already resumed")
245‚Üí            }
246‚Üí        }
247‚Üí    }
248‚Üí
249‚Üí    override fun afterCompletion(state: Any?) {
250‚Üí        // Call afterResume from afterCompletion and not vice-versa, because stack-size is more
251‚Üí        // important for afterResume implementation
252‚Üí        afterResume(state)
253‚Üí    }
254‚Üí
255‚Üí    override fun afterResume(state: Any?) {
256‚Üí        if (tryResume()) return // completed before getResult invocation -- bail out
257‚Üí        // Resume in a cancellable way because we have to switch back to the original dispatcher
258‚Üí        uCont.intercepted().resumeCancellableWith(recoverResult(state, uCont))
259‚Üí    }
260‚Üí
261‚Üí    fun getResult(): Any? {
262‚Üí        if (trySuspend()) return COROUTINE_SUSPENDED
263‚Üí        // otherwise, onCompletionInternal was already invoked & invoked tryResume, and the result is in the state
264‚Üí        val state = this.state.unboxState()
265‚Üí        if (state is CompletedExceptionally) throw state.cause
266‚Üí        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
267‚Üí        return state as T
268‚Üí    }

1. The semantic meaning - a context without a Job "has not been completed and was not cancelled yet", so it should be considered "active"
2. `CoroutineScope.isActive` which returns `true` when there's no Job
3. `CoroutineContext.ensureActive()` which does nothing when there's no Job (because "such a coroutine cannot be cancelled")

### Solution:
Changed `CoroutineContext.isActive` from:
```kotlin
public val CoroutineContext.isActive: Boolean
    get() = this[Job]?.isActive == true
```
to:
```kotlin
public val CoroutineContext.isActive: Boolean
    get() = this[Job]?.isActive ?: true
```

This makes the behavior consistent with `CoroutineScope.isActive` and aligns with the conceptual understanding that a coroutine context without a Job is always "active" because it cannot be cancelled.

### Documentation updates:
Updated the KDoc comment to reflect the new behavior:
- Added: "or the context does not have a [Job] in it"
- Updated the shortcut expression from `coroutineContext[Job]?.isActive == true` to `coroutineContext[Job]?.isActive ?: true`

### Tests:
Created a new test file `CoroutineContextIsActiveTest.kt` with tests covering:
- Empty context is active
- Context without Job is active
- Context with active Job is active
- Context with cancelled Job is not active
- Context with completed Job is not active
- Consistency with `CoroutineScope.isActive`
- Within `withEmptyContext`, `isActive` is true

public val CoroutineContext.isActive: Boolean

322‚Üí *     val flow = flow<Unit> {
323‚Üí *         coroutineContext // Resolves into the context of outer launch, which is incorrect, see KT-38033
324‚Üí *         currentCoroutineContext() // Retrieves actual context where the flow is collected
325‚Üí *     }
326‚Üí * }
327‚Üí * ```
328‚Üí */
329‚Üípublic suspend inline fun currentCoroutineContext(): CoroutineContext = coroutineContext

 15‚Üí// --------------- core job interfaces ---------------
 16‚Üí
 17‚Üí/**
 18‚Üí * A background job. Conceptually, a job is a cancellable thing with a life-cycle that
 19‚Üí * culminates in its completion.
 20‚Üí *
 21‚Üí * Jobs can be arranged into parent-child hierarchies where cancellation
 22‚Üí * of a parent leads to immediate cancellation of all its [children] recursively.
 23‚Üí * Failure of a child with an exception other than [CancellationException] immediately cancels its parent and,
 24‚Üí * consequently, all its other children. This behavior can be customized using [SupervisorJob].
 25‚Üí *
 26‚Üí * The most basic instances of `Job` interface are created like this:
 27‚Üí *
 28‚Üí * * **Coroutine job** is created with [launch][CoroutineScope.launch] coroutine builder.
 29‚Üí *   It runs a specified block of code and completes on completion of this block.
 30‚Üí * * **[CompletableJob]** is created with a `Job()` factory function.
 31‚Üí *   It is completed by calling [CompletableJob.complete].
 32‚Üí *
 33‚Üí * Conceptually, an execution of a job does not produce a result value. Jobs are launched solely for their
 34‚Üí * side effects. See [Deferred] interface for a job that produces a result.
 35‚Üí *
 36‚Üí * ### Job states
 37‚Üí *
 38‚Üí * A job has the following states:
 39‚Üí *
 40‚Üí * | **State**                        | [isActive] | [isCompleted] | [isCancelled] |
 41‚Üí * | -------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------- | ------------- |
 42‚Üí * | _New_ (optional initial state)   | `false`    | `false`       | `false`       |
 43‚Üí * | _Active_ (default initial state) | `true`     | `false`       | `false`       |
 44‚Üí * | _Completing_ (transient state)   | `true`     | `false`       | `false`       |
 45‚Üí * | _Cancelling_ (transient state)   | `false`    | `false`       | `true`        |
 46‚Üí * | _Cancelled_ (final state)        | `false`    | `true`        | `true`        |
 47‚Üí * | _Completed_ (final state)        | `false`    | `true`        | `false`       |
 48‚Üí *
 49‚Üí * Usually, a job is created in the _active_ state (it is created and started). However, coroutine builders
 50‚Üí * that provide an optional `start` parameter create a coroutine in the _new_ state when this parameter is set to
 51‚Üí * [CoroutineStart.LAZY]. Such a job can be made _active_ by invoking [start] or [join].
 52‚Üí *
 53‚Üí * A job is _active_ while the coroutine is working or until [CompletableJob] is completed,
 54‚Üí * or until it fails or cancelled.
 55‚Üí *
 56‚Üí * Failure of an _active_ job with an exception makes it _cancelling_.
 57‚Üí * A job can be cancelled at any time with [cancel] function that forces it to transition to
 58‚Üí * the _cancelling_ state immediately. The job becomes _cancelled_  when it finishes executing its work and
 59‚Üí * all its children complete.
 60‚Üí *
 61‚Üí * Completion of an _active_ coroutine's body or a call to [CompletableJob.complete] transitions the job to
 62‚Üí * the _completing_ state. It waits in the _completing_ state for all its children to complete before
 63‚Üí * transitioning to the _completed_ state.
 64‚Üí * Note that _completing_ state is purely internal to the job. For an outside observer a _completing_ job is still
 65‚Üí * active, while internally it is waiting for its children.
 66‚Üí *
 67‚Üí * ```
 68‚Üí *                                       wait children
 69‚Üí * +-----+ start  +--------+ complete   +-------------+  finish  +-----------+
 70‚Üí * | New | -----> | Active | ---------> | Completing  | -------> | Completed |
 71‚Üí * +-----+        +--------+            +-------------+          +-----------+
 72‚Üí *                  |  cancel / fail       |
 73‚Üí *                  |     +----------------+
 74‚Üí *                  |     |
 75‚Üí *                  V     V
 76‚Üí *              +------------+                           finish  +-----------+
 77‚Üí *              | Cancelling | --------------------------------> | Cancelled |
 78‚Üí *              +------------+                                   +-----------+
 79‚Üí * ```
 80‚Üí *
 81‚Üí * A `Job` instance in the
 82‚Üí * [coroutineContext](https://kotlinlang.org/api/latest/jvm/stdlib/kotlin.coroutines/coroutine-context.html)
 83‚Üí * represents the coroutine itself.
 84‚Üí *
 85‚Üí * ### Cancellation cause
 86‚Üí *
 87‚Üí * A coroutine job is said to _complete exceptionally_ when its body throws an exception;
 88‚Üí * a [CompletableJob] is completed exceptionally by calling [CompletableJob.completeExceptionally].
 89‚Üí * An exceptionally completed job is cancelled and the corresponding exception becomes the _cancellation cause_ of the job.
 90‚Üí *
 91‚Üí * Normal cancellation of a job is distinguished from its failure by the type of this exception that caused its cancellation.
 92‚Üí * A coroutine that threw [CancellationException] is considered to be _cancelled normally_.
 93‚Üí * If a cancellation cause is a different exception type, then the job is considered to have _failed_.
 94‚Üí * When a job has _failed_, then its parent gets cancelled with the exception of the same type,
 95‚Üí * thus ensuring transparency in delegating parts of the job to its children.
 96‚Üí *
 97‚Üí * Note, that [cancel] function on a job only accepts [CancellationException] as a cancellation cause, thus
 98‚Üí * calling [cancel] always results in a normal cancellation of a job, which does not lead to cancellation
 99‚Üí * of its parent. This way, a parent can [cancel] its own children (cancelling all their children recursively, too)
100‚Üí * without cancelling itself.
101‚Üí *
102‚Üí * ### Concurrency and synchronization
103‚Üí *
104‚Üí * All functions on this interface and on all interfaces derived from it are **thread-safe** and can
105‚Üí * be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without external synchronization.
106‚Üí *
107‚Üí * ### Not stable for inheritance
108‚Üí *
109‚Üí * **`Job` interface and all its derived interfaces are not stable for inheritance in 3rd party libraries**,
110‚Üí * as new methods might be added to this interface in the future, but is stable for use.
111‚Üí */
112‚Üípublic interface Job : CoroutineContext.Element {
113‚Üí    /**
114‚Üí     * Key for [Job] instance in the coroutine context.
115‚Üí     */
116‚Üí    public companion object Key : CoroutineContext.Key<Job>
117‚Üí
118‚Üí    // ------------ state query ------------
119‚Üí
120‚Üí    /**
121‚Üí     * Returns the parent of the current job if the parent-child relationship
122‚Üí     * is established or `null` if the job has no parent or was successfully completed.
123‚Üí     *
124‚Üí     * Accesses to this property are not idempotent, the property becomes `null` as soon
125‚Üí     * as the job is transitioned to its final state, whether it is cancelled or completed,
126‚Üí     * and all job children are completed.
127‚Üí     *
128‚Üí     * For a coroutine, its corresponding job completes as soon as the coroutine itself
129‚Üí     * and all its children are complete.
130‚Üí     *
131‚Üí     * @see [Job] state transitions for additional details.
132‚Üí     */
133‚Üí    @ExperimentalCoroutinesApi
134‚Üí    public val parent: Job?
135‚Üí
136‚Üí    /**
137‚Üí     * Returns `true` when this job is active -- it was already started and has not completed nor was cancelled yet.
138‚Üí     * The job that is waiting for its [children] to complete is still considered to be active if it
139‚Üí     * was not cancelled nor failed.
140‚Üí     *
141‚Üí     * See [Job] documentation for more details on job states.
142‚Üí     */
143‚Üí    public val isActive: Boolean
144‚Üí
145‚Üí    /**
146‚Üí     * Returns `true` when this job has completed for any reason. A job that was cancelled or failed
147‚Üí     * and has finished its execution is also considered complete. Job becomes complete only after
148‚Üí     * all its [children] complete.
149‚Üí     *
150‚Üí     * See [Job] documentation for more details on job states.
151‚Üí     */
152‚Üí    public val isCompleted: Boolean
153‚Üí
154‚Üí    /**
155‚Üí     * Returns `true` if this job was cancelled for any reason, either by explicit invocation of [cancel] or
156‚Üí     * because it had failed or its child or parent was cancelled.
157‚Üí     * In the general case, it does not imply that the
158‚Üí     * job has already [completed][isCompleted], because it may still be finishing whatever it was doing and
159‚Üí     * waiting for its [children] to complete.
160‚Üí     *
161‚Üí     * See [Job] documentation for more details on cancellation and failures.
162‚Üí     */
163‚Üí    public val isCancelled: Boolean
164‚Üí
165‚Üí    /**
166‚Üí     * Returns [CancellationException] that signals the completion of this job. This function is
167‚Üí     * used by [cancellable][suspendCancellableCoroutine] suspending functions. They throw exception
168‚Üí     * returned by this function when they suspend in the context of this job and this job becomes _complete_.
169‚Üí     *
170‚Üí     * This function returns the original [cancel] cause of this job if that `cause` was an instance of
171‚Üí     * [CancellationException]. Otherwise (if this job was cancelled with a cause of a different type, or
172‚Üí     * was cancelled without a cause, or had completed normally), an instance of [CancellationException] is
173‚Üí     * returned. The [CancellationException.cause] of the resulting [CancellationException] references
174‚Üí     * the original cancellation cause that was passed to [cancel] function.
175‚Üí     *
176‚Üí     * This function throws [IllegalStateException] when invoked on a job that is still active.
177‚Üí     *
178‚Üí     * @suppress **This an internal API and should not be used from general code.**
179‚Üí     */
180‚Üí    @InternalCoroutinesApi
181‚Üí    public fun getCancellationException(): CancellationException
182‚Üí
183‚Üí    // ------------ state update ------------
184‚Üí
185‚Üí    /**
186‚Üí     * Starts coroutine related to this job (if any) if it was not started yet.
187‚Üí     * The result is `true` if this invocation actually started coroutine or `false`
188‚Üí     * if it was already started or completed.
189‚Üí     */
190‚Üí    public fun start(): Boolean
191‚Üí
192‚Üí
193‚Üí    /**
194‚Üí     * Cancels this job with an optional cancellation [cause].
195‚Üí     * A cause can be used to specify an error message or to provide other details on
196‚Üí     * the cancellation reason for debugging purposes.
197‚Üí     * See [Job] documentation for full explanation of cancellation machinery.
198‚Üí     */
199‚Üí    public fun cancel(cause: CancellationException? = null)
200‚Üí
201‚Üí    /**
202‚Üí     * @suppress This method implements old version of JVM ABI. Use [cancel].
203‚Üí     */
204‚Üí    @Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
205‚Üí    public fun cancel(): Unit = cancel(null)
206‚Üí
207‚Üí    /**
208‚Üí     * @suppress This method has bad semantics when cause is not a [CancellationException]. Use [cancel].
209‚Üí     */
210‚Üí    @Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Since 1.2.0, binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
211‚Üí    public fun cancel(cause: Throwable? = null): Boolean
